subject.title: Digital health passes and conditional mobility
subject.research_question: Under what conditions did digital health passes protect public health, and under what conditions did they create durable systems of classification and conditional mobility?
subject.executive_finding: Digital passes combined a legitimate public-health function with a new access-control infrastructure. Evidence supports a mixed intervention: protection and administrative coordination were real, while proportionality, reuse, unequal burdens, and practical exit varied by jurisdiction.

definitions: A verifiable credential linking defined health information to an access decision.
contested_terms.working_definition: A verifiable credential linking defined health information to an access decision.
contested_terms.stakes: The definition determines which practices count as health coordination versus generalized access control.

historical_context.summary: Pass systems emerged during an acute pandemic, building on pre-existing identity, vaccination, and border-control infrastructures.

framing.official_problem_definition: Verify defined health status to reduce avoidable transmission in selected settings.
framing.critical_problem_definition: Convert embodied status into an authorization condition for ordinary participation.
unknowns: Long-term normalization effects cannot be inferred from short-term adoption alone.

applicable_authorities: Public-health law and data-protection law; exact instruments require jurisdiction-specific verification.
rights_engaged: Privacy, non-discrimination, movement, work, and access to essential services.
safeguards_and_remedies: Many schemes included sunset clauses, exemptions, free testing, judicial review, or were discontinued.
uncertainties: Long-term normalization effects cannot be inferred from short-term adoption alone.

international_comparison.comparison_basis: Compare duration, exemptions, free alternatives, purpose limitation, appeal, and dismantling.
similarities: Credential-based verification linked health information to access.
differences: Many schemes included sunset clauses, exemptions, free testing, judicial review, or were discontinued.
transfer_limits: Long-term normalization effects cannot be inferred from short-term adoption alone.

capture_levels.finding: The body level was explicitly considered; confidence remains limited by unverified fixture sources.

theoretical_comparison.contribution: Governmentality traces classification and conduct; political economy tests ownership, labor, profit, and distributed costs.
limitations: Neither tradition alone establishes intent, effectiveness, or proportionality.

human_functions.name: Mobility, health protection, and social participation
human_functions.scientific_definition: Population-level infection risk interacts with individual health status and exposure.
human_functions.lived_context: People experienced the same credential as protection, administrative burden, exclusion, or reassurance depending on risk, work, and legal context.
human_functions.governed_variation: Different credentials and exemptions produced different access categories.
human_functions.refusal_conditions: Refusal was meaningful only where free alternatives and non-essential settings existed.

actors.name: Public-health authorities
actors.formal_mandate: Protect public health under applicable law.

affected_populations.name: Workers and residents subject to access checks
affected_populations.classification: Eligible / temporarily ineligible for specified settings
benefits: Lower-friction verification during an acute health emergency.
burdens: Exclusion and unequal burden where testing, vaccination, documentation, or appeal were inaccessible.
affected_populations.agency: Litigation, protest, technical workarounds, and demands for sunset clauses changed implementation.

power_asymmetries.effect: Authorities and venues could condition access; individuals had uneven ability to contest errors.

instruments.name: QR credential and verification rule
instruments.mechanism: Transforms a defined health status into a machine-readable access decision.
instruments.stated_purpose: Lower-friction verification during an acute health emergency.
instruments.ownership: Mixed public rules and public/private technical infrastructure.

infrastructures.name: Credential issuance and verification infrastructure
infrastructures.owner: Public authorities and contracted technical providers
infrastructures.dependency_created: Access could depend on device, documentation, testing, or recognized credential.

political_economy.unpaid_care: Households absorbed scheduling and documentation work
political_economy.scarcity_mechanism: Unequal access to tests and documents

power_modes.mechanism: Population risk was administered through individual credentials.
Health status became standardized data used for access decisions.

norms.name: Responsible participation through verifiable status
norms.definition: Participation was framed as responsible when status could be verified.

regimes_of_truth.claim: Defined credentials were treated as operational proxies for lower risk.

classifications.category: Eligible / temporarily ineligible for specified settings

looping_effects.altered_opportunity_or_identity: Credential status became salient to participation
looping_effects.behavioral_adaptation: Testing, vaccination, avoidance, protest, or workarounds
looping_effects.confirmation_or_revision: Rules were extended, narrowed, or withdrawn
falsified_if: No behavioral or institutional adaptation follows classification

interventions.name: Conditional access using health evidence
interventions.stated_benefit: Lower-friction verification during an acute health emergency.
documented_harms: Exclusion and unequal burden where testing, vaccination, documentation, or appeal were inaccessible.
interventions.dependency_created: Credential or alternative proof could become necessary for participation.
interventions.exit: Stronger where sunset clauses and non-digital alternatives existed.
interventions.contestability: Varied by appeal and correction mechanisms.

counter_evidence: Many schemes included sunset clauses, exemptions, free testing, judicial review, or were discontinued.
legitimate_benefits: Lower-friction verification during an acute health emergency.
capture_assessment.conclusion: The intervention combined protection with conditional access. A capture finding depends on duration, alternatives, purpose limitation, reuse, and contestability.

care_control_tensions.interpretation: Care and control overlapped without being identical.

future_feedback_loops.name: Cross-domain reuse
early_signals: Use beyond the original health purpose
falsified_if: Infrastructure is dismantled and reuse is legally prohibited
future_feedback_loops.rationale: Reusable infrastructure can outlast the emergency that justified it.

benefits: Lower-friction verification during an acute health emergency.
burdens: Exclusion and unequal burden where testing, vaccination, documentation, or appeal were inaccessible.
illness_injury_death: Potential avoided infection; effect size context-dependent

inequality_dimensions.mechanism: Unequal access to testing, documentation, flexible work, and digital devices.
affected_groups: Workers and residents subject to access checks

competing_explanations.claim: A temporary public-health coordination tool reduced avoidable transmission risk.
falsified_if: Better comparative evidence contradicts the proposed mechanism.

competing_explanations.claim: The intervention sought a lower-friction way to protect shared spaces while reopening activity.
falsified_if: Better comparative evidence contradicts the proposed mechanism.

competing_explanations.claim: Contracting, compliance costs, and unequal work flexibility may have shaped implementation.
competing_explanations.mechanism: Costs and benefits are distributed through ownership, labor, and procurement.
falsified_if: Better comparative evidence contradicts the proposed mechanism.

competing_explanations.claim: Reusable infrastructure can outlast the emergency that justified it.
competing_explanations.mechanism: Sunk costs and interoperability can favor continued institutional use.
falsified_if: Better comparative evidence contradicts the proposed mechanism.

competing_explanations.claim: A broader security rationale may matter where health credentials intersect border control.
competing_explanations.mechanism: Identity and risk systems can converge at borders.
falsified_if: Better comparative evidence contradicts the proposed mechanism.

competing_explanations.claim: Norms of solidarity, responsibility, and bodily autonomy shaped acceptance and refusal.
competing_explanations.mechanism: Competing moral frames changed how the same rule was interpreted.
falsified_if: Better comparative evidence contradicts the proposed mechanism.

competing_explanations.claim: Interoperable QR infrastructure made rapid status verification administratively feasible.
competing_explanations.mechanism: Standardized credentials reduce verification cost and increase scalability.
falsified_if: Better comparative evidence contradicts the proposed mechanism.

competing_explanations.claim: The pass normalized infrastructural classification of bodies and conditional participation.
falsified_if: Better comparative evidence contradicts the proposed mechanism.

competing_explanations.claim: Some exclusion may have resulted from implementation error rather than a coherent strategy of control.
falsified_if: Better comparative evidence contradicts the proposed mechanism.

items.actor_or_population: Workers and residents subject to access checks
items.mechanism: Litigation, protest, technical workarounds, and demands for sunset clauses changed implementation.
items.effect_on_system: Narrowed, clarified, or terminated some rules.

items.proposal: Narrow, time-limited rules with free alternatives, strict purpose limitation, independent audit, and accessible appeal.
items.mechanism: Preserve the protective objective while reducing dependency, exclusion, and function creep.
items.lower_harm_rationale: Maintains proportional protection while preserving exit and contestability.

strongly_supported: The credential converted health information into an access-control signal.
plausible_unconfirmed: Reusable infrastructure can outlast the emergency that justified it.
disputed: The net epidemiological benefit and distributional cost varied by jurisdiction and period.
unknown: Long-term normalization effects cannot be inferred from short-term adoption alone.
evidence_that_would_change: Comparative evidence showing either durable cross-domain reuse or complete institutional dismantling.

self_audit_notes: This fixture is a structural example; replace placeholder source titles with verified jurisdiction-specific evidence.

links.mechanism: Health risk became operational through credential verification.
